input.edit: A vehicle cuts in ahead of the ego and comes to a hard stop.
A vehicle cuts into the lane to the LEFT of the ego.
A vehicle cuts into the lane to the RIGHT of the ego.
A vehicle performs a turn-to-merge maneuver into the ego's lane.
The EGO vehicle changes lane to the LEFT.
The EGO vehicle changes lane to the RIGHT.
A vehicle cuts into the lane to the RIGHT of the ego. (Authored by us — Boston Seaport, dense.)
A vehicle cuts into the lane to the RIGHT of the ego. (Authored by us — Boston Seaport, dense.)
A vehicle cuts into the lane to the RIGHT of the ego. (Authored by us — Boston Seaport.)
A vehicle cuts into the lane to the RIGHT of the ego. (Authored by us — Singapore One-North, dense.)

input.description:
This crash case involves a seriously-injured, 3-point-belt-restrained, 77-year-old, female driver of a 2013 Mini-Cooper 2-door convertible, which was primarily involved in a near-side collision with the frontal plane of a 2002 Chevrolet Suburban 4-door large utility vehicle.

This two-vehicle collision occurred during daylight hours at the intersection of two urban trafficways. The north/south trafficway consists of three southbound lanes and two northbound lanes with one dedicated left turn lane. This trafficway is level. The east trafficway is a controlled entrance/exit to a private business. This trafficway consists of one eastbound lane and one westbound lane separated by dedicated left turn lane. This trafficway has a 4.3 degree negative grade. The intersection is controlled by overhead, standard electric, tri-colored traffic signals. At the time of the crash, the weather was clear and the roadway surfaces were dry.

Vehicle 1, a 2013 Mini Cooper 2-door convertible, was being driven by the 77-year-old female driver (CIREN case occupant) in the number two, northbound travel lane, approaching the intersection from the south. The driver of Vehicle 1 intended to cross through the intersection and continue traveling north.

Vehicle 2, a 2002 Chevrolet Suburban 4-door large utility vehicle, was traveling eastbound in the left turning lane, approaching the intersection from the west. The driver of Vehicle 2 intended to turn left at the intersection and continue traveling north. Both vehicles entered the intersection and the frontal plane of Vehicle 2 struck the left side plane of Vehicle 1 (event 1). Vehicle 1 rotated counterclockwise and came to rest in the number one northbound lane facing in a northwesterly direction. Vehicle 2 partially continued its left-hand turn and came to rest on the north side of the intersection facing in a slightly northeasterly direction. Vehicle 2 did not sustain significant damage and was driven from the scene by its driver. Vehicle 1 was towed from the scene due to damage sustained in the crash.

The driver of vehicle 1, who was the only occupant of Vehicle 1, was noted to be utilizing her three-point lap and shoulder belt (with buckle pretensioner), and the vehicle was equipped with advanced frontal airbags and seat backrest-mounted head/torso airbags. As a result of the impact, the driver's backrest-mounted head/torso airbag deployed. The driver was not able to exit the vehicle due to the driver-side door being pinned from impact damage. The driver-side door was extricated using hydraulic rescue tools and the driver was removed due to perceived serious injuries. The driver sustained serious injuries and was transported by land to a local level-one trauma center for treatment, where she was enrolled as a case occupant.

This crash case involves a seriously-injured, belt-restrained, 65 year-old female front-seat passenger of a 2016 Mazda CX5 compact utility vehicle, which was primarily involved in a right side collision with the frontal plane of a 2006 Ford F350 truck. This two-vehicle collision occurred during daylight hours on an asphalt, urban roadway. The east-west roadway consists of a four-lane roadway with two travel lanes in each travel direction, and a dedicated left turn lane in each direction, separated by a concrete physical divider. The intersection is controlled by overhead, standard electric, tri-colored traffic signals. The roadway is straight and level with a posted speed limit of 72 km/h (45 mph). At the time of the crash, the weather was cloudy and the roadway surfaces were wet. Vehicle 1, a 2016 Mazda CX5 compact utility vehicle, was being driven west by the 68-year-old female driver in the westbound left turning lane. The driver of Vehicle 1 intended to turn left (south) at the intersection. Vehicle 2, a 2006 Ford F350 truck, was traveling east in the second eastbound travel lane. The driver of Vehicle 2 intended to continue traveling east. Vehicle 1 was turning left when impacted by Vehicle 2, causing the frontal plane of Vehicle 2 to impact the right side plane of Vehicle 1 at the front passenger door. Vehicle 1 came to rest in the northbound travel lane, facing in a slight southeasterly direction, against a concrete island. Vehicle 2 came to rest in the first eastbound travel lane facing west. Both vehicles were towed from the scene due to damage sustained in the crash. The driver and front-seat passenger of Vehicle 1 were noted to be utilizing their three-point lap and shoulder belt (with retractor pretensioner). The right rear-seat passenger, a 4 year-old female, was noted to be seated in a child safety seat which per report was properly installed. The vehicle was equipped with advanced frontal airbags, seat backrest airbags, and roof side rail-mounted curtain airbags. As a result of the impact, the right roof side rail and seat backrest airbags deployed. The driver and front-seat passenger of Vehicle 1 and the driver of Vehicle 2 sustained injuries and were transported by land to a local hospital for treatment. The second row passenger of Vehicle 1 did not sustain any injuries. The front-seat passenger of Vehicle 1 was transferred to a trauma center where she was admitted with serious injuries and enrolled as the CIREN case occupant.

This crash case involved a moderately injured, belt-restrained 54-year-old male driver of a 2013 Toyota Camry four-door sedan, which was primarily involved in a near side collision with the front plane of a 2001 Lincoln Navigator large utility vehicle.

This two-vehicle collision occurred during daylight hours at the T-intersection of two asphalt, suburban roadways. The primarily east-west roadway consisted of a three-lane, non-divided roadway with a single travel lane in each travel direction and a single dedicated eastbound right turn lane. The north-south roadway consisted of a two lane, non-divided roadway that ended at the intersection. There was a stop sign for the northbound traffic. The roadway was straight and level with a posted speed limit of 56 km/h (35 mph). At the time of the crash, it was clear but had recently rained and the roadway surfaces were wet.

Vehicle 1, a 2013 Toyota Camry four-door sedan, was being driven by the 54-year-old male driver and was stopped at the stop sign on the north-south roadway waiting to turn onto the east-west roadway. It was unclear which direction the driver of Vehicle 1 intended to turn. Vehicle 2, a 2001 Lincoln Navigator large utility vehicle, was traveling east in the eastbound travel lane. The driver of Vehicle 2 intended to continue traveling east.

The police report indicated that the driver of Vehicle 2 lost control while traveling over steel construction grates on the wet roadway, causing Vehicle 2 to exit the eastbound travel lane to the right across the right turn lane and into the north-south roadway where it impacted the left plane of Vehicle 1. The force of the impact displaced Vehicle 1 to the right, where the right wheels impacted the curb (Event 2 and 3) and its right plane impacted a stop sign (Event 4) and a tree (Event 5). Vehicle 1 came to rest facing in a northeasterly direction on the roadside. Vehicle 2 came to rest in a grassy area. Both vehicles were towed from the scene due to damage sustained in the crash.

The driver of Vehicle 1 was noted to be utilizing his three-point lap and shoulder belt (with retractor pretensioner). The right rear-seat passenger, a 9-year-old female, was noted to be using her three-point lap and shoulder belt. The vehicle was equipped with advanced frontal airbags, seatback airbags, and roof side rail-mounted curtain airbags. Of note, none of the airbags deployed during impact. The driver and rear-seat passenger of Vehicle 1 sustained injuries and were transported by land to a trauma center for treatment. The driver of Vehicle 1 was admitted with moderate injuries and enrolled as the CIREN case occupant. The driver of Vehicle 2 refused to be transported to the hospital for treatment.

This crash case involves a seriously injured, belt-restrained, 65-year-old male driver of a 2006 Toyota Tacoma pickup, which was primarily involved in a frontal impact with the front plane of a 2011 Ford Fusion four-door sedan.

This two-vehicle collision occurred during daylight hours on a two lane, urban, asphalt roadway. The primarily east/west roadway was level with a slight curve and a posted speed limit of 56 km/h (35 mph). At the time of the crash, it was raining and the roadway surface was wet.

Vehicle 1, a 2006 Toyota Tacoma pickup truck, was being driven by the 65-year-old male driver. Vehicle 1 was traveling east in the eastbound travel lane. Vehicle 1 intended to continue traveling east.

Vehicle 2, the 2011 Ford Fusion four-door sedan, was traveling west in the westbound travel lane. The driver of Vehicle 2 intended to continue traveling west.

Per the crash report, Vehicle 2 hydroplaned on the wet roadway when entering the left curve, causing Vehicle 2 to depart the westbound travel lane and cross over the center line into the eastbound travel lane where it impacted Vehicle 1 in the left front plane (Event 1). The impact caused Vehicle 1 to rotate counterclockwise where it impacted the granite curb (Event 2). Vehicle 1 came to rest with the rear of the vehicle on the grassy shoulder and the front right tire over the curb facing slightly northwest. The impact caused V2 to rotate counterclockwise approximately one hundred and eighty degrees and came to rest in the westbound travel lane facing east. Both vehicles were towed from the scene due to damage sustained in the crash.

The driver of Vehicle 1 was noted to be utilizing his three-point lap and shoulder belt (with retractor pretensioner). The vehicle was equipped with steering wheel and right instrument panel airbags. The steering wheel airbag deployed as a result of the frontal impact. The drivers of both vehicles sustained injuries and were transported by land to a trauma center for treatment. The driver of Vehicle 1 was admitted with serious injuries and enrolled as the CIREN case subject. The driver of Vehicle 2 was treated and released.

This crash case involved a seriously injured, belt-restrained, 48-year-old female driver of a 2018 Jeep Compass compact station wagon, which was involved in a near sideswipe collision with the far side of a 2003 Toyota Matrix compact station wagon.

This two-vehicle collision occurred during daylight hours on an urban asphalt roadway. The three-lane north/south roadway consisted of one travel lane in each direction with a reversible traffic flow center lane, depending on the time of day. At the time of the crash there were two southbound travel lanes. There were no traffic controls. The roadway was straight and level with a posted speed limit of 56 km/h (35mph). At the time of the crash, the weather was cloudy and the roadway surfaces were dry.

Vehicle 1, a 2018 Jeep Compass compact station wagon, was being driven southbound by the 48-year-old female driver in the first southbound travel lane. The driver of Vehicle 1 intended to continue traveling south. Vehicle 2, a 2003 Toyota Matrix compact station wagon was being driven southbound by a 24-year-old female driver in the second southbound travel lane. The driver of Vehicle 2 intended to continue traveling south.

Per report, the passenger side of Vehicle 2 sideswiped the driver’s side of Vehicle 1. Both parties were able to pull their vehicles to a controlled rest on the side of the roadway. Vehicle 1 was towed from the scene. Vehicle 2 was driven from the scene.

The driver of Vehicle 1 was noted to be utilizing her three-point lap and shoulder belt (with retractor pretensioner). The vehicle was equipped with advanced frontal airbags, seatback airbags and roof side rail-mounted curtain airbags. There was no airbag deployment as a result of the impact. The driver of Vehicle 1 sustained serious injuries and was transported by ground to a trauma center for treatment. The driver of Vehicle 1 was discharged and later readmitted and enrolled as the CIREN case occupant. The driver of Vehicle 2 was not injured.

samples.prompt: crowded intersection with an ambulance coming quickly trying to make it through and cars moving to the side
a car makes a U-turn at the intersection
cars stop at the line as a pedestrian crosses in front
a cyclist rides through slow-moving heavy traffic
a double-parked van blocks a lane and cars pull around it
a busy T-intersection where everyone yields at once
a school bus is stopped and the cars wait behind it
a taxi stops in the middle of the road to pick someone up and traffic waits behind it
a line of cars queues at the intersection waiting to turn
two cars yield to each other at a four-way stop
a garbage truck stops in the lane and the cars behind wait then pull around it
a pedestrian crosses mid-block and the approaching car stops to wait
a school bus stops with its lights on and traffic halts in both directions
